title: 1,400 Dead in Venezuela. 27 Countries Are Digging.
script: Sixty-eight thousand still missing? Two magnitude 7 earthquakes — 39 seconds apart — struck Venezuela on June 24, killing more than 1,430 people and leaving 68,900 unaccounted for. It's the worst earthquake disaster in Venezuelan recorded history. Caracas and La Guaira took the worst of it. Buildings collapsed mid-block. Residents dug with their hands because the government never sent heavy equipment. The golden 72-hour window for finding survivors alive passed days ago. Then on Sunday, rescue workers pulled two 11-year-old boys out of the rubble — days after the quake. Teams from 27 countries are now on the ground with 140 search dogs and 2,245 specialists. The USGS model still says the final toll could reach 100,000. So which of those 68,900 people just hasn't been found yet?
